---
title: "Final Project of Divvy Bikes"
author: "Ning Xie"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        % Enable wrapping for Pandoc code blocks
        \usepackage{fvextra}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{makecell}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{commandchars=\\\{\}}
editor: source
execute-dir: project
execute:
  echo: true
---

```{python}
# setup
import geopandas as gpd
from os.path import join
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import altair as alt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import time
import warnings
import seaborn as sns
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
alt.renderers.enable('png')
data_path = 'data/external'

# improve graph resolution
import tempfile
from IPython.display import SVG, display, Image
import vl_convert as vlc

def display_altair_png(chart, scale=2):
    """
    Render an Altair chart to a PNG and display it inline.

    Parameters
    ----------
    chart : altair.Chart
        Altair chart object to render.
    scale : int, optional
        Resolution scaling factor for the PNG (default = 2). Use scale=2 for standard slides. Use scale = 3–4 for dense figures or PDF exports.
    """
    png_bytes = vlc.vegalite_to_png(chart.to_dict(), scale=scale)

    # Write to a temporary PNG file and display
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".png") as tmp:
        tmp.write(png_bytes)
        tmp.flush()
        display(Image(filename=tmp.name))
```

```{python}
#| eval: False
#| echo: True
# Data process for Divvy Bike
# Calculate riding time
divvy_2506 = pd.read_csv('data_final/202506-divvy-tripdata.csv')

divvy_2506['started_at'] = pd.to_datetime(divvy_2506['started_at'])
divvy_2506['ended_at'] = pd.to_datetime(divvy_2506['ended_at'])

divvy_2506['duration_min'] = (divvy_2506['ended_at'] - divvy_2506['started_at']).dt.total_seconds() / 60.0

# Calculate riding distance
start_points = gpd.GeoSeries(
    gpd.points_from_xy(divvy_2506['start_lng'], divvy_2506['start_lat']),
    crs="EPSG:4326"
)

end_points = gpd.GeoSeries(
    gpd.points_from_xy(divvy_2506['end_lng'], divvy_2506['end_lat']),
    crs="EPSG:4326"
)

start_proj = start_points.to_crs(epsg=32616)
end_proj   = end_points.to_crs(epsg=32616)

divvy_2506['distance_km'] = start_proj.distance(end_proj) / 1000

# Find out time distribution

divvy_2506['start_hour'] = divvy_2506['started_at'].dt.hour
divvy_2506['end_hour']   = divvy_2506['ended_at'].dt.hour

divvy_2506['day_of_week'] = divvy_2506['started_at'].dt.dayofweek  # 0=Mon

DAY_NAMES = ['Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thu', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun']

divvy_2506['day_name'] = divvy_2506['day_of_week'].map(dict(enumerate(DAY_NAMES)))

divvy_2506['week_part'] = divvy_2506['day_of_week'].apply(lambda x: 'Weekend' if x>=5 else 'Weekday')

divvy_2506.head()

```

```{python}

# Store to derive_data folder as Parquet (smaller & faster)
#divvy_2506.to_parquet(
    "data_final/derive_data/divvy_202506_cleaned.parquet",
    #compression="snappy",
   # index=False）

print("Saved to derive_data/divvy_202506_cleaned.parquet")
```

```{python}
# Draw Maps

divvy_2506 = pd.read_parquet("data_final/derive_data/divvy_202506_cleaned.parquet")

# --- 1) Build small aggregated DataFrames ---
bike_counts = (
    divvy_2506['rideable_type']
    .value_counts()
    .reset_index())

bike_counts['label'] = bike_counts['rideable_type'].map({
    'electric_bike': 'Electric Bike',
    'classic_bike': 'Classic Bike'
}).fillna(bike_counts['rideable_type'])

member_counts = (
    divvy_2506['member_casual']
    .value_counts()
    .reset_index())

member_counts['label'] = member_counts['member_casual'].map({
    'member': 'Member',
    'casual': 'Casual'
}).fillna(member_counts['member_casual'])

# ensure consistent order Weekday then Weekend
week_counts = (
    divvy_2506['week_part']
    .value_counts()
    .reindex(['Weekday','Weekend'])
    .reset_index()
)
week_counts['label'] = week_counts['week_part']

# --- 2) color map (edit hex if you want) ---
colors = {
    'Electric Bike': '#FF9800',
    'Classic Bike': '#2196F3',
    'Member': '#4CAF50',
    'Casual': '#E91E63',
    'Weekday': '#2b8cbe',
    'Weekend': '#8da0cb'
}

# helper: create a bar chart with labels (bars + text)
def bar_with_labels(df_small, cat_col='label', val_col='count', title=''):
    base = alt.Chart(df_small).encode(
        x=alt.X(f'{cat_col}:N', sort='-y', title=None,
        axis=alt.Axis(labelAngle=0)),
        y=alt.Y(f'{val_col}:Q', title='Number of Trips'),
        tooltip=[alt.Tooltip(f'{cat_col}:N', title=cat_col), alt.Tooltip(f'{val_col}:Q', title='Count', format=',')]
    )

    bars = base.mark_bar().encode(
        color=alt.Color(f'{cat_col}:N',
        scale=alt.Scale(domain=list(colors.keys()), range=list(colors.values())),
                        legend=None)
    ).properties(
        width=260, height=360, title=title
    )

    texts = base.mark_text(dy=-10, fontWeight='bold', fontSize=14).encode(
        text=alt.Text(f'{val_col}:Q', format=',')
    )

    return (bars + texts)

# --- 3) Build the three charts ---
chart_bike = bar_with_labels(bike_counts,   cat_col='label', val_col='count', title='Bike Type')

chart_member = bar_with_labels(member_counts, cat_col='label', val_col='count', title='Membership Type')

chart_week = bar_with_labels(week_counts,   cat_col='label', val_col='count', title='Weekday vs Weekend')

# Layout and styling
final = (chart_bike | chart_member | chart_week).configure_title(
    fontSize=16, fontWeight='bold'
).configure_axis(
    labelFontSize=12, titleFontSize=13
).properties(
    # add an overall title by embedding in a text chart if you want,
    # or show them as-is and rely on individual titles.
    #width=260
)

# Display (in notebook/Quarto this renders inline)
final

png_bytes = vlc.vegalite_to_png(final.to_dict(), scale=3)

with open("time_member_analysis/fig_bar_pattern.png", "wb") as f:
    f.write(png_bytes)
```

```{python}
# Build small aggregated tables (start-hour)
bike_small = (
    divvy_2506.dropna(subset=['start_hour', 'week_part', 'rideable_type'])
      .groupby(['week_part', 'start_hour', 'rideable_type'], as_index=False)
      .size().rename(columns={'size': 'count'})
)

member_small = (
    divvy_2506.dropna(subset=['start_hour', 'week_part', 'member_casual'])
      .groupby(['week_part', 'start_hour', 'member_casual'], as_index=False)
      .size().rename(columns={'size': 'count'})
)

# -------------------------------
# NEW: normalize by exposure days
# -------------------------------
DAY_DIVISOR = {'Weekday': 21, 'Weekend': 9}
bike_small['avg_per_day']   = bike_small['count'] / bike_small['week_part'].map(DAY_DIVISOR)
member_small['avg_per_day'] = member_small['count'] / member_small['week_part'].map(DAY_DIVISOR)

# Map pretty labels
bike_map = {'electric_bike': 'Electric Bike', 'classic_bike': 'Classic Bike'}
member_map = {'member': 'Member', 'casual': 'Casual'}
bike_small['rideable_label'] = bike_small['rideable_type'].map(bike_map).fillna(bike_small['rideable_type'])
member_small['member_label'] = member_small['member_casual'].map(member_map).fillna(member_small['member_casual'])

# Set color scales
bike_colors = alt.Scale(domain=['Electric Bike','Classic Bike'], range=['#FF9800','#2196F3'])
member_colors = alt.Scale(domain=['Member','Casual'], range=['#4CAF50','#E91E63'])


# ===============================
# Chart A: Bike Type
# ===============================
chart_bike = alt.Chart(bike_small).mark_bar(opacity=0.85).encode(
    column=alt.Column('week_part:N', title=None, sort=['Weekday', 'Weekend']),
    x=alt.X('start_hour:O', title='Hour of Day', axis=alt.Axis(labelAngle=0)),
    y=alt.Y('avg_per_day:Q', title='Avg rides per day'),

    color=alt.Color('rideable_label:N', title='Bike Type',
                    scale=bike_colors),

    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip('week_part:N', title='Week Part'),
        alt.Tooltip('start_hour:Q', title='Hour'),
        alt.Tooltip('rideable_label:N', title='Bike Type'),
        alt.Tooltip('avg_per_day:Q', title='Avg/day', format=',.1f')
    ],
).properties(
    width=300, height=280, title='Start Time Distribution — Bike Type'
)


# ===============================
# Chart B: Membership
# ===============================
chart_member = alt.Chart(member_small).mark_bar(opacity=0.85).encode(
    column=alt.Column('week_part:N', title=None, sort=['Weekday', 'Weekend']),
    x=alt.X('start_hour:O', title='Hour of Day', axis=alt.Axis(labelAngle=0)),
    y=alt.Y('avg_per_day:Q', title='Avg rides per day'),

    color=alt.Color('member_label:N', title='Membership',
                    scale=member_colors),

    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip('week_part:N', title='Week Part'),
        alt.Tooltip('start_hour:Q', title='Hour'),
        alt.Tooltip('member_label:N', title='Membership'),
        alt.Tooltip('avg_per_day:Q', title='Avg/day', format=',.1f')
    ],
).properties(
    width=300, height=280, title='Start Time Distribution — Membership'
)


# ===============================
# Final layout
# ===============================
final_hist = alt.vconcat(chart_bike, chart_member).resolve_scale(
    color='independent'
).configure_axis(
    labelFontSize=11, titleFontSize=12
).configure_legend(
    titleFontSize=11, labelFontSize=11
)

display(final_hist)

png_bytes = vlc.vegalite_to_png(final_hist.to_dict(), scale=3)

with open("time_member_analysis/fig_start_time_pattern.png", "wb") as f:
    f.write(png_bytes)

print("Saved to time_member analysis/fig_start_time_pattern.png")

```

```{python}
plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')
plt.rcParams['figure.dpi'] = 200
plt.rcParams['savefig.dpi'] = 200
plt.rcParams['axes.unicode_minus'] = False

BIKE_COLORS = {'classic_bike': '#2196F3', 'electric_bike': '#FF9800'}
MEMBER_COLORS = {'member': '#4CAF50', 'casual': '#E91E63'}

df_dur  = divvy_2506[(divvy_2506['duration_min']>0) & (divvy_2506['duration_min']<=120)]
df_dist = divvy_2506[(divvy_2506['distance_km']>0) & (divvy_2506['distance_km']<=30)]

# ========= ONE FIGURE =========
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 9))

# -------------------------------
# 1. Duration by Bike
ax = axes[0,0]
for b in ['classic_bike','electric_bike']:
    subset = df_dur[df_dur['rideable_type']==b]['duration_min']
    ax.hist(subset, bins=60, alpha=0.6,
            label=b.replace('_',' ').title(),
            color=BIKE_COLORS[b])
ax.set_title("Duration by Bike Type")
ax.set_xlabel("Minutes")
ax.set_ylabel("Trips")
ax.legend()

# -------------------------------
# 2. Duration by Membership
ax = axes[0,1]
for m in ['member','casual']:
    subset = df_dur[df_dur['member_casual']==m]['duration_min']
    ax.hist(subset, bins=60, alpha=0.6,
            label=m.title(),
            color=MEMBER_COLORS[m])
ax.set_title("Duration by Membership")
ax.set_xlabel("Minutes")
ax.legend()

# -------------------------------
# 3. Distance by Bike
ax = axes[1,0]
for b in ['classic_bike','electric_bike']:
    subset = df_dist[df_dist['rideable_type']==b]['distance_km']
    ax.hist(subset, bins=60, alpha=0.6,
            label=b.replace('_',' ').title(),
            color=BIKE_COLORS[b])
ax.set_title("Distance by Bike Type")
ax.set_xlabel("Kilometers")
ax.set_ylabel("Trips")
ax.legend()

# -------------------------------
# 4. Distance by Membership
ax = axes[1,1]
for m in ['member','casual']:
    subset = df_dist[df_dist['member_casual']==m]['distance_km']
    ax.hist(subset, bins=60, alpha=0.6,
            label=m.title(),
            color=MEMBER_COLORS[m])
ax.set_title("Distance by Membership")
ax.set_xlabel("Kilometers")
ax.legend()

# overall
fig.suptitle("Duration and Distance", fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()

# save + display
plt.savefig("time_member_analysis/fig_all_distributions.png")
plt.show()
plt.close()
```
